python3 -c "
import json
with open('articles/2026/03/15/articles.jsonl') as f:
    for line in f:
        a = json.loads(line)
        title = (a.get('title','') or '').lower()
        content = (a.get('content','') or '').lower()[:5000]
        for kw in ['arsenal champions league', 'ucl quarter draw', 'champions league draw', 'round of 16 result']:
            if kw in title or kw in content[:2000]:
                print(f'[{kw}] {a.get(\"title\",\"\")[:160]}')
                print(a.get('content','')[:500])
                print('---')
                break
" 2>/dev/null | head -30

blocks.content:
[central israel] Cars were burned and overturned by shrapnel in Tel Aviv district
Cars were seen burned and one overturned in a town in Tel Aviv district caused by shrapnel after a wave of Iranian missiles struck central Israel. According to local police, three people were wounded.
---

Cars were seen burned and one overturned in a town in Tel Aviv district caused by shrapnel after a wave of Iranian missiles struck central Israel. According to local police, three people were wounded.

[central israel] Iran war: What is happening on day 16 of US-Israel attacks?
Israel and US attacks on Isfahan kills 15 people as Tehran carries out retaliatory attacks on Israel and Gulf countries.
Share
The United States and Israel carried out attacks on Iran’s Isfahan city in the early hours of Sunday, killing at least 15 people, as the conflict entered its 16th day.
Sirens blared in central Israel on Sunday as Iran launched multiple barrages of missiles in retaliatory attacks on the country. Tehran has continued its attacks in the Gulf countries.
Recommended Stories

with open('articles/2026/03/15/articles.jsonl') as f:
    for line in f:
        a = json.loads(line)
        title = (a.get('title','') or '').lower()
        content = (a.get('content','') or '').lower()[:5000]
        if 'tel aviv' in content[:3000] and ('killed' in content[:3000] or 'dead' in content[:3000] or 'two people' in content[:3000]) and 'missile' in content[:3000]:
            print(f'{a.get(\"title\",\"\")[:160]}')
            print(a.get('content','')[:500])

blocks.content:
Loud Blasts In Dubai, Kuwait Airport Hit In Fresh Iranian Strikes
New Delhi:
Loud sounds were heard in Dubai's Marina and Al Sufouh areas, while operations at UAE's Fujairah oil terminal came to a halt after Iranian strikes on Sunday as the Middle East war entered the 16th day. Further, several drones struck Kuwait's international airport, but no injuries were reported.
Clouds of dark black smoke rose from Fujairah, as authorities battling the fire for hours at a stretch, adding that a Jordanian national had been injured.
The latest sounds came after successfu
---
Transcript: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," March 15, 2026
The following is the transcript of the interview with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on March 15, 2026.
MARGARET BRENNAN: Charlie D'Agata in Tel Aviv. We turn now to the Foreign Minister of Iran Abbas Araghchi. Good evening to you, sir.
FOREIGN MINISTER ABBAS ARAGHCHI: Well, good evening to you. Thank you for having me for the second time.
MARGARET BRENNAN: Yes, and they are extraordinary times. Minister, President Trump said this we
---
In West Asia, a futile war that serves no ends
Iran has amply proved the point that 5,000-year-old civilisations don’t fold up in a few days of bombing. On the war’s opening day, Iran’s Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Hosseini Khamenei, was killed along with 44 of the regime’s top leaders. The US and Israel thought that, like Iraq, people would come out on the streets in big numbers to overthrow the regime.
But the reverse happened.
Hundreds of thousands of people took to the streets to mourn his death. Then, Iran retaliated against the US, We
---
